document_title: LLM Harness Engineering: Market and Breakpoint Analysis

reference.notes: Selected example is a VC market-framing piece (b2venture) useful for taxonomy and wedge thinking but must be supplemented with primary-source evidence and traction data.

figures.title: LLM Harness Stack — Development → Runtime → Ops
figures.purpose: Conceptual layered view showing developer frameworks, runtime/orchestration, and ops/observability; supports the Market map section.

figures.title: Market Map — Maturity vs Operational Complexity
figures.purpose: Positioning map to identify categories that are operationally complex but immature (candidate wedges).

figures.title: Provider Landscape — Category Comparison
figures.purpose: High-level comparison of core categories and exemplar providers; supports the Provider landscape section.

figures.title: Technical Bottlenecks — Solved → Partially Solved → Hard
figures.purpose: Visual classification of technical problems by maturity to support the Technical bottlenecks section.

figures.title: Breakpoint / Wedge Positioning
figures.purpose: Positioning of candidate newcomer wedges by feasibility and defensibility; supports Breakpoint analysis.

figures.title: Executive Summary — Top Wedges & Recommendation
figures.purpose: Compact visual used in the Executive Summary to orient readers to the recommended wedge and market map.

sections.title: Executive summary
sections.summary: Concise summary of definition, state, top providers, gaps, top 3 wedges, recommended wedge.

illustration_plan.purpose: Orient readers quickly to the market layers and recommended wedge

sections.title: Market map and taxonomy
sections.summary: Define LLM harness engineering and present a structured taxonomy with evidence and boundaries.

illustration_plan.purpose: Conveys layered stack and candidate insertion points for startups

sections.title: Provider landscape
sections.summary: Detailed, evidence-backed provider analysis organized by category (commercial and open-source).

illustration_plan.purpose: Category comparison visual to support provider analysis

sections.title: Customer and JTBD analysis
sections.summary: User personas, jobs-to-be-done, buying criteria, and frustrations.

illustration_plan.purpose: Support JTBD mapping with a simple category/persona overlay

sections.title: Technical bottlenecks
sections.summary: Analyze solved, partially solved, and unsolved technical problems.

illustration_plan.purpose: Classify technical problems by maturity and visualize hardest gaps

sections.title: Commercial landscape and moat analysis
sections.summary: Where value accrues, defensibility assessment, role of open source.

illustration_plan.purpose: Positioning map showing value accrual vs commoditization risk

sections.title: Breakpoint analysis and ranked wedges
sections.summary: Ranked newcomer wedges with evidence and risk assessment.

illustration_plan.purpose: Visual ranking of candidate wedges by feasibility & defensibility

sections.title: Strategic recommendations
sections.summary: Product recommendation, GTM, roadmap, metrics for PMF.

illustration_plan.purpose: Highlight recommended wedge and roadmap commentary

sections.title: Appendix: sources and comparison matrices
sections.summary: Source list, company table, comparison matrix, terminology notes.
sections.evidence_plan: Populate core provider rows (LangChain, LlamaIndex, Langfuse, LangSmith, OpenAI Evals, Promptfoo, Pinecone, Anthropic, Datadog) with GH metrics, funding/traction, integration-spike artifacts and verified access dates/quotes.
